title: Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass, an American Slave
author: Douglass, Frederick
summary: Frederick Douglass’s Narrative recounts his escape from Maryland slavery and emergence as a powerful abolitionist speaker. In the Preface Wendell Phillips praises Douglass’s eloquence, moral courage, and public impact after his Nantucket and New Bedford appearances. The Narrative details institutionally inflicted brutality — scourgings, mutilations, and mental degradation — and the debasement of slave and enslaver alike. Douglass’s reflective longing for freedom on Chesapeake Bay, his soliloquies to receding ships, and his resolve to rise from fetters to lecturing agent are highlighted. The work condemns slavery as execrable, educates northern audiences, and urges immediate emancipation and human compassion and justice.

characters.name: Frederick Douglass
characters.age: adult (estimated 20s-30s at time of escape and lecturing in text)
characters.lifespan: Escaped slavery, became a fugitive lecturer and public advocate for abolition (as described in the Preface)

characters.name: Wendell Phillips
characters.lifespan: Preface author who meets and publicly praises Douglass; advocates for his protection

characters.name: John A. Collins
characters.lifespan: General Agent of the Massachusetts Anti-Slavery Society who urged Douglass to lecture

characters.name: Charles Lenox Remond
characters.personality: eloquent orator, representative of free colored population
characters.lifespan: Named as a fellow black abolitionist and noted public speaker

characters.name: Daniel O'Connell
characters.lifespan: Quoted in the Preface for an anecdote and his view on slavery's brutalizing tendency

characters.name: Patrick Henry
characters.lifespan: Referenced by Phillips as a comparison to Douglass's eloquence

characters.name: Beloved friend from New Bedford (unnamed)
characters.lifespan: Encouraged Douglass to address the Nantucket convention and aided his public debut

characters.name: An American sailor (unnamed)
characters.personality: victim in anecdote, formerly rational then degraded
characters.lifespan: Cast away and enslaved in Africa for three years in O'Connell's anecdote; found 'imbruted and stultified'

characters.name: The driver (unnamed)
characters.lifespan: Figure referenced as administering lashings and enforcing labor under slavery

locations.name: Approximate Mason and Dixon's Line (referenced boundary)

unusual_words.definition: shackled or fettered, bound with metal restraints
unusual_words.context: Used to describe 'manacled brethren' suffering under slavery

unusual_words.definition: brothers or fellow members of a group (archaic/formal)

unusual_words.definition: the state of being in bondage or slavery (archaic)
unusual_words.context: Describes the oppressive bondage from which the enslaved seek deliverance

unusual_words.definition: breathing hard, figuratively yearning eagerly
unusual_words.context: Describes 'manacled brethren ... panting for deliverance'

unusual_words.definition: devoted or dedicated formally to a purpose
unusual_words.context: Phillips urges Douglass to 'consecrate his time and talents' to abolition

unusual_words.definition: a person with extraordinary talents
unusual_words.context: Douglass is called a 'prodigy' in natural eloquence

unusual_words.definition: addressing something abstract or absent as if present
unusual_words.context: Douglass 'apostrophizing' receding ships as symbols of freedom

unusual_words.definition: an earnest expression of protest or remonstrance
unusual_words.context: Preface references 'expostulation' as part of the Narrative's moral pleadings

unusual_words.definition: extremely bad or detestable
unusual_words.context: Used to condemn slavery as an 'execrable' system

unusual_words.definition: those who assist or encourage wrongdoing
unusual_words.context: Refers to those who support or enable slavery as 'abettors'

unusual_words.definition: made brutish or degraded (archaic/rare)
unusual_words.context: O'Connell's anecdote describes a castaway 'imbruted and stultified'

unusual_words.definition: made ineffective or foolish; deprived of ability
unusual_words.context: Describes mental degradation in the Anecdote: 'imbruted and stultified'

unusual_words.definition: nonsensical or unintelligible language

unusual_words.definition: to make brutal or dehumanize

unusual_words.definition: chains or shackles used to restrain
unusual_words.context: Douglass 'broke his iron fetters' upon escaping slavery

unusual_words.definition: one who trades or deals, here trading in people
unusual_words.context: Phillips condemns those who act as 'trafficker in slaves and the souls of men'

unusual_words.definition: a listening audience or hearing space (somewhat uncommon)
unusual_words.context: Douglass's speech affected a 'crowded auditory'

unusual_words.definition: well chosen or pleasing; apt
unusual_words.context: Phillips praises Douglass's 'felicitous remarks'

unusual_words.definition: optimistic or hopeful (somewhat literary)
unusual_words.context: Douglass's success exceeded 'the most sanguine expectations'

unusual_words.context: Phillips sought to 'instil hope and courage' into Douglass

unusual_words.definition: shyness or lack of self-confidence
unusual_words.context: Douglass expressed 'unfeigned diffidence' about public speaking

unusual_words.definition: guidance, support, or patronage

unusual_words.definition: quiet, gentle submissiveness (old-fashioned tone)
unusual_words.context: Phillips praises Douglass's 'meekness' balanced with manliness

unusual_words.definition: those who make false statements to damage someone's reputation

unusual_words.definition: narrow-mindedness or intolerance
unusual_words.context: Phillips condemns the 'illiberality of spirit' among critics

unusual_words.definition: seeming plausible but actually wrong; deceptively attractive

unusual_words.definition: extremely ugly or offensive
unusual_words.context: O'Connell calls slavery 'still hideous' despite disguises

unusual_words.definition: the ending of a period of time
unusual_words.context: At the 'expiration of that period' the sailor was found degraded

unusual_words.definition: making humane or civilizing
unusual_words.context: Ironically used about 'The Domestic Institution' as a supposed humanizing force

unusual_words.definition: to read thoroughly or carefully (somewhat literary)
unusual_words.context: Phillips challenges those who can 'peruse' the Narrative without feeling

unusual_words.definition: too great or intense to be expressed in words
unusual_words.context: Used in 'unutterable abhorrence of slavery'

unusual_words.definition: unfeeling or hard-hearted (metaphorical)
unusual_words.context: Phillips says only a 'flinty heart' could remain unmoved by the Narrative

unusual_words.definition: deserving strong condemnation; lamentable
unusual_words.context: Describes Douglass's 'deplorable' situation under slavery

unusual_words.definition: punishments or corrections (archaic tone)
unusual_words.context: Refers to the 'terrible chastisements' inflicted upon his person

unusual_words.definition: acts of extreme cruelty or violence

unusual_words.context: The passage on Chesapeake Bay is praised for its 'sublimity'

unusual_words.definition: strong desires to achieve something high or great
unusual_words.context: Douglass's 'sublime aspirations' are noted despite bondage

unusual_words.definition: thoughtful, given to deep thought
unusual_words.context: 'as he grew reflective and intelligent' his misery augmented

unusual_words.context: Describes 'what perils he encountered' in attempts to escape

unusual_words.definition: efforts or attempts
unusual_words.context: Discusses his 'endeavors to escape from his horrible doom'

unusual_words.definition: notable or remarkable
unusual_words.context: 'how signal have been his deliverance'—remarkable preservation

unusual_words.context: Refers to Douglass's 'deliverance' from slavery

unusual_words.definition: protection from harm or decay

unusual_words.definition: merciless; showing no pity

unusual_words.definition: speaking one's thoughts aloud when alone

unusual_words.definition: full of life or spirit; given life to (figurative)

unusual_words.definition: relating to Alexandria, here implying vast learning
unusual_words.context: 'a whole Alexandrian library of thought' compressed into the passage

unusual_words.definition: an earnest or humble request
unusual_words.context: The passage contains 'entreaty' among other appeals

unusual_words.definition: an expression of sharp disapproval or criticism

unusual_words.definition: to bury or hide deeply

unusual_words.definition: having four feet; used to liken humans to animals
unusual_words.context: Reduces humans 'to a level with four-footed beasts'

unusual_words.definition: unwilling or unable to believe something

unusual_words.definition: a detailed account or narrative
unusual_words.context: People disbelieve any 'recital of the cruelties' of slavery

unusual_words.definition: whippings or flogging punishments (archaic term)

unusual_words.definition: the act of depriving of a limb or disfiguring

unusual_words.definition: made greater in size or intensity

unusual_words.definition: rising and falling, as with emotion
unusual_words.context: Phillips says the Narrative can leave the reader with a 'heaving breast'

unusual_words.definition: affected by pain or distress
unusual_words.context: Readers might have an 'afflicted spirit' reading the Narrative

unusual_words.definition: morally right or justifiable (religious tone)
unusual_words.context: Appeal to a 'righteous God' in the Preface

unusual_words.definition: acknowledged or admitted

unusual_words.definition: distinctive or unusual
unusual_words.context: Phillips says Douglass's experience 'was not a peculiar one'

unusual_words.definition: an example or sample

unusual_words.definition: carried out (usually of a harmful act)

unusual_words.definition: professing: claiming or asserting (often hypocritically)

unusual_words.definition: qualities traditionally associated with men: courage, strength
unusual_words.context: Douglass is noted for 'manliness of character' alongside meekness

unusual_words.definition: having high moral standards
unusual_words.context: Phillips praises Douglass's 'virtuous traits of character'

unusual_words.definition: lasting or enduring
unusual_words.context: His 'ever-abiding remembrance' of those in bonds is noted

unusual_words.definition: approval or praise expressed especially by clapping

unusual_words.definition: a feeling of self-consciousness, shame, or awkwardness
unusual_words.context: He displayed 'hesitancy and embarrassment' when first speaking

unusual_words.definition: new or unusual in an interesting way
unusual_words.context: The path Douglass would take was 'wholly an untrodden one' and 'novel' to him

unusual_words.definition: to tell or recount a story or series of events
unusual_words.context: He 'proceeded to narrate some of the facts in his own history as a slave'

unusual_words.definition: anger or annoyance provoked by perceived unfair treatment
unusual_words.context: Douglass's eloquence could 'rouse ... virtuous indignation' in listeners

unusual_words.definition: feelings of pity and sorrow for someone else's misfortune
unusual_words.context: Phillips notes Douglass secured 'sympathy and affection' from friends

unusual_words.definition: the relation of size or amount between things
unusual_words.context: Describes Douglass's 'physical proportion and stature commanding and exact'

unusual_words.definition: precise or accurate
unusual_words.context: His physical proportion was described as 'commanding and exact'

unusual_words.definition: provided with a quality, ability, or asset
unusual_words.context: He was 'in intellect richly endowed' according to the Preface

unusual_words.definition: the faculty of forming new ideas or images
unusual_words.context: Phillips asserts nothing in the Narrative is 'drawn from the imagination'

unusual_words.definition: the intention or desire to do evil; ill will
unusual_words.context: Phillips says 'nothing has been set down in malice' in the Narrative

unusual_words.definition: a person or thing that adorns or adds honor
